channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Watch live: Gabbard makes case to lead national intelligence at confirmation hearing
item.description: Former Hawaii Rep. Tulsi Gabbard (D-Hawaii) is testifying Thursday morning before the Senate Select Committee on Intelligence to make her case as President Trump's pick for director of national intelligence.
item.dc:creator: The Hill Staff, NewsNation

item.title: Watch live: Kash Patel faces senators at confirmation hearing
item.description: Kash Patel, President Trump's controversial nominee to head the FBI, will face senators Thursday morning during a confirmation hearing.
item.dc:creator: The Hill Staff, NewsNation

item.title: President Trump sounds the same. His White House
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The marathon Q&A sessions are back, along with the cream Oval Office rug and the Diet Coke button on the Resolute Desk. So, too, are the late-night social media posts that ricochet across the globe and the barrage of executive orders.
item.dc:creator: JILL COLVIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: By the numbers: ICE daily enforcement updates provide arrest, detainer statistics
item.description: U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) is issuing daily updates detailing the number of individuals arrested and detainers lodged as part of immigration enforcement efforts.
item.dc:creator: Holly Galvan Posey, KPRC 2

item.title: Criminal records of Jan. 6 rioters pardoned by Trump include rape, domestic violence
item.description: Dozens of Jan. 6 defendants who received pardons from President Donald Trump had past criminal convictions for charges including rape, manslaughter, domestic violence and drug trafficking.
item.dc:creator: Tom Dreisbach, MPR News

item.title: Despite Trump immigration policy changes, experts say the H-2A program is on solid footing
item.description: The Trump administration has started its overhaul of existing immigration policy. However, one program that allows migrant agricultural workers to work temporarily is for now, unaffected.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Thamer, MPR News

item.title: Sparks expected to fly at Kash Patel’s Senate confirmation hearing to lead FBI
item.description: Kash Patel is set to join the Senate Judiciary Committee on Thursday morning to face his nomination hearing to serve as the second Trump administration's FBI director.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump order cracks down on antisemitism and could deport foreign student protesters
item.description: The executive order directs government agencies to use all available tools to prosecute or remove perpetrators of antisemitic harassment and violence, especially on college campuses.
item.dc:creator: Tovia Smith, MPR News

item.title: Trump's immigration crackdown: Some proposals may put California at odds with Washington
item.description: Senate Bill 54 is California's so-called sanctuary law, which limits local law enforcement from assisting with federal immigration efforts.
item.dc:creator: Jenny Huh, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump administration revokes deportation protections for 600,000 Venezuelans
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Homeland Security Secretary Kristi Noem said Wednesday that the Trump administration has revoked a decision that would have protected roughly 600,000 people from Venezuela from deportation, putting some at risk of being removed from the country in about two months.
item.dc:creator: REBECCA SANTANA and GISELA SALOMON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What is the Laken Riley Act? A look at the first bill Trump just signed
item.description: President Donald Trump on Wednesday signed the first bill of his new administration, and it is named after a slain Georgia nursing student whose name became a rallying cry during his White House campaign.
item.dc:creator: ADRIANA GOMEZ LICON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump orders preparation of Guantánamo Bay facility to house migrants
item.description: President Trump signed a memo on Wednesday to prepare a massive facility at Guantánamo Bay to be used to house deported migrants. Trump had earlier teased he intended to do so during a signing ceremony for an immigration-related bill.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Democrats demand Patel information in special counsel's classified documents report
item.description: Democrats on the Senate Judiciary Committee are demanding to see the volume of former special counsel Jack Smith’s report dealing with the Mar-a-Lago classified documents investigation, asserting it has relevant information about Kash Patel, President Trump’s pick to lead the FBI.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: White House budget office rescinds federal funding freeze memo
item.description: The Trump administration’s Office of Management and Budget released a new memo Wednesday rescinding a controversial memo issued late Monday that froze a wide swath of federal financial assistance, which had paralyzed many federal programs and caused a huge uproar on Capitol Hill.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump’s DOJ could shift key Supreme Court cases
item.description: President Trump’s Justice Department could change the department’s position on critical Supreme Court cases, potentially altering the trajectory of high-profile appeals before the justices.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, NewsNation

item.title: Harris team planned Houston rally with Joe Rogan in mind
item.description: Former Vice President Kamala Harris made a substantial effort to get on Joe Rogan’s popular podcast amid concerns about her traction with male voters — going so far as to plan a Houston rally to justify a stop at Rogan’s Austin studio, according to a forthcoming book.
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, NewsNation

item.title: RFK Jr. pressed on past comments about vaccines at HHS confirmation hearing
item.description: If approved, Kennedy will control a $1.7 trillion agency that oversees food and hospital inspections, vaccine recommendations and more.
item.dc:creator: Tom Dempsey, NewsNation

item.title: A Democratic Party in crisis will elect its new chairman on Saturday
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The Democratic National Committee will elect a new chair on Saturday as the party seeks new leadership to guide Democrats through Donald Trump's second presidency. Eight candidates have lined up to replace outgoing Chair Jaime Harrison.
item.dc:creator: STEVE PEOPLES, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Trump invites Israel's Netanyahu to meet with him at the White House next week
item.description: WADI GAZA, Gaza Strip (AP) — President Donald Trump has invited Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu to the White House next week as the first foreign leader to visit in Trump's second term, Netanhayu and the White House said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and MELANIE LIDMAN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Florida congresswoman wants Trump's face to be carved on Mount Rushmore
item.description: The Mount Rushmore National Memorial in South Dakota currently features the 60-foot-tall heads of four U.S. presidents.
item.dc:creator: Sierra Rains, NewsNation

item.title: Trump administration to offer buyouts to all federal workers ahead of return to office
item.description: The Trump administration is offering all 2 million federal employees what amounts to a buyout if they do not intend to return to work in person later this year, sources confirmed to The Hill.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, WFLA, NBC

item.title: States lose access to Medicaid payment portal amid Trump federal aid freeze
item.description: State Medicaid programs across the country are reporting they've lost access to federal payment portals one day after President Trump announced a freeze on federal grants and aid.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Choi, Fox 5

item.title: Capitol Police arrest man with Molotov cocktails allegedly targeting GOP politicians
item.description: Officers said they found a folding knife “as well as two destructive devices from the inside pockets of ENGLISH’s jacket” when they searched Ryan English.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, NewsNation

item.title: What to know about White House press secretary Karoline Leavitt
item.description: Karoline Leavitt, 27, is the youngest person to hold the position of press secretary since Ron Ziegler, who worked as President Nixon’s top press official.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, NewsNation

item.title: Trump issues order to ban transgender troops from serving openly in the military
item.description: The executive order speaks of transgender identity in sweeping and dismissive terms, and sets the stage for a policy that is more restrictive and punitive than the ban from Trump’s first term.
item.dc:creator: Selena Simmons-Duffin, MPR News

item.title: Voters to receive postcards for District 1 special primary election
item.description: Registered voters in San Diego County's First Supervisorial District will soon receive postcards for the April 8 special primary election.
item.dc:creator: Domenick Candelieri, Fox 5

item.title: Nevada Republicans pass Democrats in voter registration, PAC reports
item.description: Nevada Republicans said Monday they have passed Democrats in voter registration in the state for the first time since 2007.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Florida legislative leaders respond to DeSantis attacks, imply he didn't read TRUMP Act or is lying
item.description: Florida's congressional leaders fired back at Gov. Ron DeSantis after he accused of them of "theatrics" Monday.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Rodriguez, WFLA, NBC

item.title: NJ congressman launches plan to tackle rising meat and egg prices
item.description: FORT LEE, N.J. (PIX11) -- Food prices in the coming year are expected to continue to increase, according to the Department of Agriculture. It's unlikely to be the sharp rise we saw in the past few years, but certain products such as eggs and meats continue to rise more than most other groceries.
item.dc:creator: Henry Rosoff, PIX 11

item.title: White House says Colombia will take deported migrants after Trump showdown
item.description: The White House said Sunday night that a U.S.-Colombia agreement had come together in the wake of a back-and-forth between the two countries over topics including immigration and tariffs.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, Fox 5

item.title: Gabbard's path through Intelligence Committee narrows ahead of key hearing
item.description: Republicans are cautioning that Tulsi Gabbard’s path to confirmation to lead the U.S. intelligence apparatus is narrowing as she seemingly has trouble winning over key GOP senators. Gabbard, along with Robert F. Kennedy Jr.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, Fox 5

item.title: Florida schools will comply with ICE on campuses: Education department
item.description: Schools across America, including in Florida, are gearing up for the possibility of officials with Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) showing up to their campuses.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump ‘very surprised’ by Murkowski, Collins votes against Hegseth
item.description: President Donald Trump said Friday that he wasn't expecting Republican Sens. Lisa Murkowski (Alaska) and Susan Collins (Maine) to vote against his controversial nominee for secretary of Defense, Army veteran and former Fox News host Pete Hegseth.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5
